name: Driving Licence
description: An official document issued by the Regional Transport Office (RTO) authorizing an individual to drive a motor vehicle in India.
estimated_processing_time: Learner's: Immediate after test, Permanent: 15-30 days
application_fee: Learner's Licence: ₹200, Permanent Licence: ₹200, Driving Test: ₹300
eligibility: 16+ for gearless 2-wheelers (with consent), 18+ for light motor vehicles.
application_steps: Visit the Parivahan Sarathi portal and select your state.
Apply for a Learner's Licence (LL) first. Fill the form and upload documents.
Book an appointment for the LL test or take it online via Aadhaar e-KYC.
Pass the test and download your Learner's Licence.
After 30 days of holding the LL, apply for a Permanent Driving Licence.
Book a slot for the driving test at the RTO.
The DL will be dispatched to your registered address.

required_documents.name: Proof of Age
required_documents.purpose: To verify the applicant meets the legal driving age.
required_documents.where_to_get: Birth Certificate, PAN Card, Class 10 Marksheet, Passport

required_documents.name: Proof of Address
required_documents.where_to_get: Aadhaar Card, Passport, Voter ID, LIC Policy

required_documents.name: Form 1 and Form 1A (Medical Certificate)
required_documents.purpose: To declare physical fitness (Form 1A mandatory for 40+ or transport vehicles).

common_mistakes: Applying for a permanent DL before the 30-day Learner's period ends.
Failing to carry original documents to the RTO for verification.
Using an improper vehicle for the driving test.
